violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2026

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. Establishment must maintain daily Sanitizer Logs. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026
The pestle (of the mortar and pestle) was observed to be wrapped in tape, impacting its ability to be cleaned and sanitized, as it is no longer smooth. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the knife rack, and cold drawers have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. Wall-mounted fan has an accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The area near the dump sink in the cooking area is in poor repair along the wall, with a large crack impacting its ability to be cleaned and sanitized. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2026

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. Owner arrived during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024
The hand-washing sink in the toilet room was lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 06/19/2024

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of soil residue and biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by an accumulation of large rodent droppings directly outside the kitchen door. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Proof of pest control service to be provided at Reinspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024
Food observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024

An open bin of clean towels observed stored on the floor beneath the hand sink in the kitchen. Clean equipment, utensils, and linens must be stored in a clean dry location, where they are not subject to dust, splash, or other contamination, and at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024
The ice bin was not stored inverted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonia sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink and sanitizer buckets. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the wall-mounted fan have an accumulation of dust and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024
The door to the toilet room in the kitchen is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 06/19/2024
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024
The dumpster area has an accumulation of leaf litter and debris.The dumpster area must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024

The areas outside the back door, along the building, and behind the walk in cooler have an accumulation of litter, leaf litter, debris, and rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024
The walk in fan cover has an accumulation of dust and mold. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024

The floor in the walk in cooler has an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024
